name: Payervand, Kiana

evidence: Kiana Payervand of Dallas, TX (LinkedIn lists Dallas, Texas); a student, prior Highland Park High School (HPHS) and UT. Same/related household (Roozbeh Payervand) also appears in Austin municipal campaign-finance filings; Kiana is recorded giving to Austin candidate Vanessa Fuentes, consistent with a repeat small-dollar Austin local donor rather than any policy-advocacy actor. No federal PAC records and no lobbyist registration.

name: Thompson, Kacey
evidence: Kacey Thompson is a Lead Teacher in the Bridging Kindergarten program at Good Shepherd Episcopal School in Austin (matches 78756 area and 'Teacher' occupation). Filing also lists a Covenant Presbyterian Church tie. No federal PAC contributions and no Texas lobbyist registration found.

name: Bufkin, Susan
evidence: Susan Bufkin of Austin 78731 (4005 Edgefield Ct, matches zip), self-describes as self-employed / mother / homemaker under 'Bufkin Brothers'. Married to Ben Bufkin, a real-estate developer on Endeavor Real Estate Group's operating committee (co-led The Domain). No distinct 'Bufkin Brothers' business entity documented publicly; her own occupation is homemaker/self-employed, so industry classified Self-Employed (real-estate wealth is via spouse). Couple are documented supporters of Big Brothers Big Sisters of Central Texas. No federal PAC records and no lobbyist registration for Susan.

name: Hawkins, Mark
evidence: Mark Lee Hawkins, attorney at Armbrust & Brown, PLLC, 100 Congress Ave Ste 1300, Austin 78701 (matches zip and 'Legal' occupation). Practice is commercial/real-estate/construction litigation and mediation (member, National Academy of Distinguished Neutrals); acts as general counsel for Austin condominium/mixed-use developments. Despite A&B's reputation as a land-use/zoning lobbying firm, Hawkins is a litigator/mediator and does NOT appear on the Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registry (2021-2026 lists checked; the John M. Hawkins and Albert Hawkins III entries are different people). No notable federal PAC contributions identified.

name: Noll, Dana
evidence: Dana Noll (SHRM-CP) is VP Human Resources / HRBP Clinical Operations at US Acute Care Solutions, based in Austin, TX. Matches donor occupation (VP Human Resources), employer (USACS), and location exactly.

name: Albert, David

Albert, PhD (Middle East Studies/Government, UT Austin), adjunct professor of government at Austin Community College since 2008, and a longtime officer of the ACC AFT union (Local 6249) - served as President, COPE Chair, and Adjunct Faculty Vice President. evidence: Self-employed jewelry importer/retailer in Austin 78746 (Westlake area). No public bio, business page, LinkedIn, FEC record, or lobbyist registration located for this specific person; classification is occupation/employer-fallback only.

name: King, David
evidence: David (Austin) King, litigation attorney at Armbrust & Brown, PLLC in Austin 78756; practice focuses on real property, land use, and construction litigation. UVA Law 2012, UT Austin B.A. 2006. Past President (2020) of Austin Young Lawyers Association. Distinct from the same-named Austin neighborhood activist. No personal TX lobbyist registration found (the firm registers other attorneys as lobbyists); no FEC PAC contributions found.

affiliations.snippet: Firm bio lists King as President of the Austin Young Lawyers Association (2020) and a board member 2015-2022, plus Austin Bar Association Civil Litigation Section board (2024-present).

name: Goff, Eric
evidence: Eric Goff, founder/president of Goff Policy, an Austin energy & infrastructure policy advocacy/consulting firm (founded 2019); also serves as Office of Public Utility Counsel residential-consumer representative at ERCOT. Prominent Austin YIMBY/urbanist housing advocate: AURA contributor (26 blog posts since 2019) and described in news as an AURA board member; vocal supporter of the HOME initiative, ADU/backyard-cottage legalization, and zoning reform. Not currently listed on AURA's board page (past/former role). No FEC PAC contributions or personal TX lobbyist registration confirmed.

affiliations.role: Board member / prolific contributor (housing & transit advocacy)
affiliations.snippet: AURA author archive shows Goff joined Aug 2019 and authored 26 posts on housing affordability, ADU/backyard-cottage legalization, transit, and desegregation; news coverage (Next City, The Real Deal) refers to him as an AURA board member commenting on the HOME/ADU reforms.

affiliations.org: ERCOT / Office of Public Utility Counsel (OPUC)
affiliations.role: Residential-consumer representative at ERCOT; chaired ERCOT subcommittees
affiliations.snippet: Goff Policy bio: Eric Goff serves as the OPUC representative for Texas residential consumers at ERCOT and has chaired ERCOT subcommittees; prior energy roles at Citigroup Energy, NRG, Reliant, and Constellation.

name: Stanford, Jason
resolved_employer: Austin ISD (Chief of Communications and Community Engagement)
evidence: Well-known Austin figure. Longtime Democratic political consultant/strategist, author (co-author of 'Forget the Alamo'), former communications director for Mayor Steve Adler; since April 2021 the Chief of Communications and Community Engagement at Austin ISD. Zip 78751 and AISD employer match the public record.

affiliations.role: Founder & principal (political research and crisis communications firm)
affiliations.snippet: Professional Democratic strategist and commentator; founded Stanford Research (1997) and Stanford Caskey (2013); credited with helping elect or reelect 30+ members of Congress and managed Chris Bell's 2006 TX gubernatorial campaign.

OpenSecrets maintains a profile for Jason Stanford as a Democratic campaign consultant; no personal federal PAC donations surfaced in FEC/OpenSecrets individual-contributor records, and he does not appear on the TEC registered-lobbyist lists.
